perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Subject to Section 8.3, Distributor's right to market, distribute and sell the Products in the Territory shall be exclusive. Distributor shall not sell any Products to a customer if Distributor knows or has reason to believe that such customer intends to remove those Products from the Territory. Distributor acknowledges and agrees that STAAR retains the right to sell the Products in the Territory directly or indirectly, including but not limited to via electronic commerce (without compensation to the Distributor). The Distributor shall cooperate with STAAR in servicing corporate accounts for customers operating in multiple Territories.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Subject to Section 8.3, Distributor's right to market, distribute and sell the Products in the Territory shall be exclusive, except that STAAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors within the Territory at its sole discretion. Distributor shall not sell any Products to a customer if Distributor knows or has reason to believe that such customer intends to remove those Products from the Territory. Distributor acknowledges and agrees that STAAR retains the right to sell the Products in the Territory directly or indirectly, including but not limited to via electronic commerce (without compensation to the Distributor). The Distributor shall cooperate with STAAR in servicing corporate accounts for customers operating in multiple Territories.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except that STAAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors within the Territory at its sole discretion,' a contradiction is introduced. The first part of the sentence states that the Distributor's right is exclusive, while the added clause gives STAAR the right to appoint other distributors, thus undermining the exclusivity previously stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Subject to Section 8.3, Distributor's right to market, distribute and sell the Products in the Territory shall be exclusive, except that STAAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors within the Territory at its sole discretion. Distributor shall not sell any Products to a customer if Distributor knows or has reason to believe that such customer intends to remove those Products from the Territory. Distributor acknowledges and agrees that STAAR retains the right to sell the Products in the Territory directly or indirectly, including but not limited to via electronic commerce (without compensation to the Distributor). The Distributor shall cooperate with STAAR in servicing corporate accounts for customers operating in multiple Territories.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Distributor's right to market, distribute, and sell the Products in the Territory shall be exclusive. The changed text adds 'except that STAAR reserves the right to appoint additional distributors within the Territory at its sole discretion,' directly contradicting the previously stated exclusivity. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as exclusivity, by definition, means not shared. The Contradicted Text is a mirror of the Changed text, so the contradiction is there. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the clause is placed with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 14.3 Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 3 above, either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon no less than ninety (90) days' prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.3 Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 3 above, either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon no less than thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party, provided that the distributor has met their Minimum Product Quantities. If the Minimum Product Quantities have not been met, then the termination notice period shall be ninety (90) days.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides a 90-day notice period for termination without cause. The addition of 'upon no less than thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party, provided that the distributor has met their Minimum Product Quantities. If the Minimum Product Quantities have not been met, then the termination notice period shall be ninety (90) days' introduces a conditional termination notice period based on Minimum Product Quantities, thereby contradicting the initial flat 90-day notice period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.3 Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 3 above, either party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon no less than thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party, provided that the distributor has met their Minimum Product Quantities. If the Minimum Product Quantities have not been met, then the termination notice period shall be ninety (90) days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a conditional termination notice period (30 or 90 days based on Minimum Product Quantities), directly contradicting the initial unqualified 90-day notice period stipulated in both the original text and contradicted text (which is the same). This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the termination notice period, thus creating a contradiction.
